backend/main.mo: Motoko actor implementing IELTS test management backend with CRUD operations for tests and student responses, authentication for admin operations, automated scoring logic, and blob storage integration for media (images/audio).
feature_evidence.json: Feature evidence report documenting four requirements: removing Next button, displaying questions in continuous vertical layout, maintaining reading test two-panel layout, and updating progress tracking for the new display mode.
frontend-file-summaries.txt: Pre-existing summaries of frontend components describing test management UI, question display, authentication, media upload, timer, scoring, and layout components with proper overflow handling.
frontend/index.css: Tailwind CSS configuration with OKLCH color system defining design tokens for light and dark modes with neutral backgrounds and accent colors.
frontend/index.html: HTML entry point with root div and module script reference for the React application titled 'IELTS Test Management by Sayem'.
frontend/src/App.tsx: Main React component managing application state (view modes: welcome/test/results/responses) and coordinating between TestManagementPanel sidebar and RightPanelContainer with header and footer.
frontend/src/components/AuthModal.tsx: Dialog component for username/password authentication with form validation, error handling, loading states, and callbacks for successful login to enable test creation.
Dialog component for creating new tests with form fields for title, type, instructions, and questions. Handles validation, submission via mutation hook, and displays errors.
frontend/src/components/Footer.tsx: Simple footer component displaying attribution text 'Powered By Sayem Ahtesham'.
Form component for building individual test questions with support for different question types, options, and media uploads (audio/image). Handles file upload progress and validation.
frontend/src/components/ReadingTestLayou: Two-panel layout component for reading tests displaying the passage on the left and all questions on the right with scrollable areas.
frontend/src/components/ResponseDetailsP: Detailed view component for a single student response showing student info, test details, scores, band score, and individual question results with export functionality.
frontend/src/components/ResponseViewer.t: Table view component for browsing all student responses with search functionality, displaying summary info, and actions to view details or delete responses.
frontend/src/components/ResultsPanel.tsx: Results display component showing test completion statistics including score, IELTS band, accuracy, time taken, and toggleable correct answers view.
frontend/src/components/RightPanelContai: Container component that conditionally renders TestInterface, ResultsPanel, ResponseViewer, or a welcome screen based on the current app view mode and state.
frontend/src/components/StudentRegistrat: Modal dialog that collects student name and batch number before allowing test start, with validation and keyboard support.
frontend/src/components/TestInterface.ts: Main test-taking interface that handles student registration, timer, fullscreen mode, answer collection, and test submission with different layouts for reading vs other test types.
frontend/src/components/TestManagementPa: Left sidebar panel for managing tests with search/filter functionality, test list display, and buttons to create new tests or view responses (requires authentication for test creation).
frontend/src/components/TestQuestionDisp: Renders individual test questions with appropriate input controls (radio buttons, text inputs, textareas) based on question type, and displays associated media (audio/image) if present.
frontend/src/hooks/useActor.ts: React Query hook that creates and manages the backend actor instance based on Internet Identity authentication state, invalidating dependent queries when identity changes.
frontend/src/hooks/useAuth.ts: Session-based authentication hook with hardcoded credentials stored in sessionStorage, providing login/logout functions and credential retrieval.
frontend/src/hooks/useFullscreen.ts: Hook that manages fullscreen mode state and provides functions to enter/exit fullscreen, with event listeners for fullscreen changes.
frontend/src/hooks/useInternetIdentity.t: Provides React context and hooks for Internet Identity authentication, managing login state, identity storage, and delegation validation with configurable AuthClient options.
frontend/src/hooks/useQueries.ts: Defines React Query hooks for CRUD operations on tests and student responses, using the backend actor and managing cache invalidation.
frontend/src/hooks/useResponseManagement: Provides filtering logic for student responses based on student name search term.
frontend/src/hooks/useTestFiltering.ts: Manages test filtering state and logic for search term and test type filters.
frontend/src/hooks/useTestForm.ts: Manages test creation form state including questions, validation, and conversion between form data and backend Test type with media support.
frontend/src/hooks/useTestSubmission.ts: Handles test submission logic including answer grading, band score calculation, and creating student response records.
frontend/src/hooks/useTestTimer.ts: Provides timer functionality with start, pause, reset controls and formatted time display for test-taking sessions.
frontend/src/index.css: Defines Tailwind CSS configuration with custom CSS variables for light and dark theme colors using OKLCH color space.
frontend/src/main.tsx: Application entry point that sets up React Query, Internet Identity provider, and renders the root App component.
frontend/tailwind.config.js: Tailwind configuration extending default theme with custom colors, animations, and design tokens for the application's design system.
project_state.json: Project state file tracking an IELTS Test Management application with features for removing the Next button and displaying all test questions in a continuous vertical layout. Contains four existing features related to question navigation and display, plus one pending feature request.
